name: Base Color Pack
description: 10 colors for the base INSEC (Input Security) Exosuit MK 0

name: INSEC Exosuit MK C "Founders"
description: Before the First Descent and the creation of Input Security as a vocation, space cargo crews used their regular issue exosuits when accompanying the mining freighters to The Well. While hardy, these suits were not tested for orbital drop, nor the extreme conditions of the protoplanets in The Well. After the First Descent, INSEC forces returning from The Well provided field-tested data which drove development on the original exosuit, which was nicknamed 'Founders' when it finally got it's final classification of Mark C.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK M "Hightower"
description: The Mark M Exosuit was the first designed after the Second Descent. Millitary optics, more durable armoring, and the ability to withstand the extreme conditions of Binary-locked protoplanets were among the first innovations added to the Mk C class of INSEC suits.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK I "Tortoise"
description: The Tortoise update to the base INSEC exosuit sported better armor on the back, which was well suited to protecting from falling debris on planets with accretion discs and high gravitational pull.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK II "Skylark"
description: The Mark II exosuit improved upon orbital drop stability, a common complaint from the first wave of INSEC forces after the First Descent.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK III "Nobility"
description: Shortly before the Second Descent, overconfident oligarchs and their families accompanied INSEC forces to The Well, to get a taste of adventure. While they insisted on being treated no different than other mercs, they were easily identified by their Mark III exosuits.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK IV "Glacier"
description: The Glacier exosuit was popular among INSEC forces dropping onto protoplanets that orbited on the outer-most rings of a locked system, with surface temperatures typically being a degree or two above absolute zero.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK V "Shade"
description: Mark V of the Exosuit came early on in expeditions, when mercs needed to blend in with the surroundings of darker planets, deflecting light in an attempt to fool the sensors of the mining bots.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK VI "Cinder"
description: The Mark VI suit was issued to INSEC forces deployed to binary-locked protoplanets circling tightly to their protostar in The Well. Temperatures top 40,000 C on some of these worlds. Mercs who opted for these conditions and higher pay did so at great cost; the materials used in the thermal shielding for the Cinder exosuit were extremely toxic.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK VII "Gilded"
description: Ironically, the Gilded exosuit, being covered with gold, was one of the most inexpensive exosuits that Corporostates produced between the First Descent and the Second Descent. Once Fyridium vapor was discovered, and manufacturing processes using crystallized Fyridium were developed, gold became a crude and worthless material.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK VIII "Reptile"
description: The Mark VIII exosuit was a short-lived experiment by the corporostate Genesync Worldwide, to determine if exosuit manufacturing could be moved to space near the edge of The Well. After a year of trial and error, it proved too costly, when the total loss of manufacturing barges during Shifts were taken in account.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK IX "Vengeance"
description: A limited run of exosuits, the Mark IX 'Vengeance' were developed after the Second Descent. These suits afforded mercs the latest in stealth, durability, and mobility. But mostly, the 'Vengeance' platform was created to warn rival corporostate INSEC forces that you had survived the Second Descent, were well-funded, well-equipped, and were not to be trifled with.

name: INSEC Exosuit MK X "Ageless"
description: The Ageless livery commemorates the deployment of Marquis Holdings Unlimited INSEC forces. Latecomers to the Fyridium trade, Marquis attracted INSEC forces with newer equipment and better payouts to families in the eventual case of merc's deaths.
